messages.content:
# Today's Lists — Monday, April 6, 2026

## SCHEDULE AT A GLANCE

**Current time: 9:44 AM** — Core academics block in progress.

---

## JP (Age 14, 9th Grade)
- [ ] Morning Scripture & Examination of Conscience (5 min)
- [ ] Latin: *Wheelock's* Chapter 18 — new forms & translation exercises (30 min)
- [ ] History: *Abridged Montanus* — Easter Monday reflection & assigned reading (25 min)
- [ ] Composition: Rhetorical analysis of Easter liturgy passage (40 min)
- [ ] Independent study block: Logic or elective (1 hour)
- [ ] *Peer teaching*: Help Joseph with grammar parsing if needed (20 min)

**Assignments to complete:**
- Finish Latin translations
- First draft of composition (due Wednesday)
- One page of notes from assigned history reading

---

## Joseph (Age 12, 7th Grade)
- [ ] Morning prayer with family
- [ ] Grammar: Sentence diagramming (three complex sentences) (25 min)
- [ ] History: Easter story in *The Narnia Chronicles* (read-aloud with Lauren or independent) (30 min)
- [ ] Natural Philosophy: Spring observations — nature journal entry (20 min)
- [ ] Math: [*Topic pending — confirm with Lauren*] (30–40 min)
- [ ] Handwork/Art: Easter-themed copywork or illumination (15 min)

**Assignments to complete:**
- Diagram sentences neatly
- Nature journal observation (with sketch)
- One short paragraph narration on history reading

---

## Michael (Age 5, Kindergarten/Early Grammar)
- [ ] Morning circle time with family
- [ ] Phonics: Letter sounds & simple word building (15 min)
- [ ] Read-aloud: *A Child's Garden of Verses* or Easter picture book (15 min)
- [ ] Numbers: Counting & simple addition to 10 (10 min)
- [ ] Handwriting: Three lines of large letters (pencil grip practice) (10 min)
- [ ] Nature play: Outdoor time & observation (30 min)
- [ ] Free play/artistic exploration (balance of structured + unstructured)

**No formal "assignments"** — narration and play are his curriculum.

---

## James (13 months)
*Observation note:* Likely practicing new mobility. Monitor readiness for more structured floor time activities by mid-year.

---

## NOTES FOR LAUREN

1. **Easter Monday tone:** This is a liturgical feast day. Consider opening with the *Victimae Paschali laudes* or a short Easter hymn before academics begin.

2. **Batching:** Joseph's math and Michael's phonics can happen during independent-work time if you're managing James. JP can work alone on Latin/composition.

3. **Peer teaching:** JP is ready to help Joseph with grammar if Joseph gets stuck. This reinforces JP's own understanding of syntax.

4. **Flexibility:** If James needs extra attention this morning, move Michael's handwriting to this afternoon and extend his outdoor time.

---

**Any adjustments or clarifications needed?**
I have some questions about our curriculum and how to improve it.
